meaning: n.盲目因袭的态度,因循守旧
examples: After decades of deprivation and conformism, Chinese consumers regard expensive consumer goods as trophies (prize) of success.
In particular, he wants to turn what has hitherto been viewed by business as a downside of Berkeley's culture-its radical non-conformism-into a virtue.
PEOPLE love to mock the middle class. Its narrow-mindedness, complacency and conformism are the mother lode of material for sitcom writers and novelists.
The Roman Catholic church has long been a byword for conformism, but bishops complain privately that the degree of centralisation has increased during his reign.
